question: How does user authentication work in FormFit, including sign in, sign up and tracking the logged-in user?
reference_answer: Authentication is delegated to Supabase. SupabaseService (app/core/services/supabase.ts) creates the Supabase client from environment config. AuthService (app/core/services/auth.ts) calls client.auth.signUp, signInWithPassword and signOut, and tracks the user in a currentUser$ BehaviorSubject where undefined means initializing, null means logged out and a User means logged in. On init it restores the server session, loads the profile and workout plan, and listens to onAuthStateChange. The login and signup components call AuthService.

question: How do route guards control navigation and the onboarding flow?
reference_answer: Functional CanActivateFn guards in app/core/guards gate routes. authGuard waits for AuthService.waitUntilReady, redirects to /login if there is no user and to /questionnaire if the profile's questionnaire_completed is false. questionnaireGuard is the inverse, sending users who already completed it to /training. noAuthGuard keeps logged-in users away from /login and /signup, and nativeBlockGuard blocks certain routes on the native build. They are attached via canActivate in app.routes.ts.

question: How is routing configured and which components are lazy-loaded?
reference_answer: app.routes.ts defines the Routes array with loadComponent dynamic imports, so every page (landing, login, signup, questionnaire, onboarding-complete, training, pricing, reset-password and the wildcard not-found) is a lazy-loaded standalone component, each guarded as needed. app.config.ts provides the router via provideRouter(routes), HttpClient, zone change detection and the global ErrorHandler.

question: How does the toast notification system work?
reference_answer: ToastService (app/core/components/toast/toast.service.ts) holds a toasts$ BehaviorSubject of a Toast array and exposes show, success, error and info; each toast is auto-dismissed after a duration via setTimeout, with errors shown longer. The Toast component (toast.ts), which uses an inline template, subscribes to toasts$ and renders the stack styled by type (success, error, info).

question: How is the user profile loaded and updated, and how are avatar images handled?
reference_answer: ProfileService (app/core/services/profile.service.ts) loads the row from the Supabase user_profiles table into a profile$ BehaviorSubject and updates it, patching the in-memory subject so the UI reacts immediately. Avatars are uploaded to the Supabase avatars storage bucket: the file extension is derived from the MIME type rather than the client filename for safety, previous avatar files are purged, and a cache-busted public URL is stored in avatar_url. The UserProfile interface (app/core/models/user-profile.model.ts) types the profile including plan tier, goals and questionnaire_completed.

question: How are subscription upgrades and payments handled?
reference_answer: PaddleService (app/core/services/paddle.service.ts) wraps Paddle.js v2 loaded via index.html. It lazily initializes Paddle with the client token and environment, opens checkout for a price id attaching the Supabase user id as customData, and on the checkout.completed event maps the price id to a plan tier (pro or lifetime) and calls ProfileService.updateProfile to upgrade the user, then navigates to /training and shows the upgrade modal. The pricing page and plan-picker service trigger checkout.

question: How are unhandled runtime errors handled application-wide?
reference_answer: GlobalErrorHandler (app/core/services/global-error-handler.ts) implements Angular's ErrorHandler and is registered in app.config.ts as the ErrorHandler provider. It ignores chunk-loading errors (handled by the router), shows a friendly error toast through ToastService inside NgZone.run, and logs the error to the console only when not in production.

question: How does the interactive body map for selecting muscle groups work?
reference_answer: The body-map feature renders an interactive anatomical map. The BodyMap component (app/features/training/body-map/body-map.component.ts) lets the user select muscle groups, using the MuscleGroup model (app/core/models/muscle-group.model.ts) and the muscle-group service (app/core/services/muscle-group.ts) that supplies the muscle-group data which drives training and exercise selection.

question: How is a user's workout plan loaded and managed?
reference_answer: WorkoutPlanService (app/core/services/workout-plan.service.ts) loads the user's plan by user id from Supabase on login and clears it on logout, exposing it as an observable; the WorkoutPlan model (app/core/models/workout-plan.model.ts) types it. WorkoutProgressService (app/core/services/workout-progress.service.ts) tracks workout progress and streaks. AuthService triggers loadPlan when a user becomes available.

question: How is the Angular application bootstrapped and what is the root component?
reference_answer: main.ts calls bootstrapApplication with the root App component and appConfig to start the standalone application. The App root component (app/app.ts) hosts the router-outlet and global UI such as the toast container and header. appConfig (app/app.config.ts) supplies the providers, including the router and the global error handler.

question: How are exercises modeled and how are they fetched and filtered for a muscle group?
reference_answer: ExerciseService (app/core/services/exercise.ts) loads the exercise catalogue from the static /data/exercises.json file via HttpClient and exposes getExercisesByMuscleGroup, which filters by muscleGroupId and, when the user supplies available equipment, keeps only exercises whose equipment intersects the allowed set (always including bodyweight, mapping machines to machine). The Exercise interface (app/core/models/exercise.model.ts) types each exercise with fields such as equipment, difficulty, steps, commonMistakes, videoUrl and isPremium.

question: How is the main training page composed and how does it choose which session to show?
reference_answer: TrainingPage (app/features/training/training-page/training-page.ts) is a standalone OnPush component that subscribes to WorkoutPlanService.getPlan() and ProfileService.getProfile(); when a plan arrives it picks the default session via SessionSelectorService.pickDefaultSession (app/features/training/session-selector.service.ts), which prefers today's scheduled non-rest session and otherwise the first training day. It hosts the BodyMap, PlanSwitcher, WorkoutMode, MobileMuscleModal and ExerciseDetail children, detects a preview query parameter, adapts to mobile viewports and evaluates the workout streak.

question: How does the active workout-mode walkthrough work?
reference_answer: WorkoutModeComponent (app/features/training/workout-mode/workout-mode.ts) takes a WorkoutSession and an isOpen flag as inputs and steps the user through the session's exercises one at a time, tracking currentIndex with next/prev (also bound to the left and right arrow keys) and a progress percentage. It loads full Exercise details from ExerciseService into a Map, emits workoutCompleted and closed events, and traps keyboard focus inside the modal using the FocusTrap utility (app/utils/focus-trap.ts).

question: How does the application header handle the user menu, plan display and logout?
reference_answer: The Header component (app/core/components/header/header.ts) waits for AuthService.waitUntilReady (app/core/services/auth.ts), then loads the current user and the ProfileService profile to show the display name, avatar and plan tier. It toggles a user menu and a mobile menu (closing them on outside clicks), opens the profile modal and the plan picker, exposes the native-platform flag via Capacitor, and logs out by calling AuthService.signOut and navigating to the landing route.

question: How does the onboarding questionnaire collect the user's details and where are they stored?
reference_answer: Questionnaire (app/features/questionnaire/questionnaire/questionnaire.ts) is a five-step standalone form collecting full name, age, gender, measurements and units, experience level, primary goal, training days, location and available equipment into a typed QuestionnaireData object. It pre-fills the name from the authenticated user's metadata and, on completion, persists the data to the user profile through ProfileService (app/core/services/profile.service.ts) and triggers plan generation via WorkoutPlanService, marking the questionnaire complete so the route guards let the user into the app.

question: How does the reset-password screen set a new password?
reference_answer: ResetPassword (app/features/reset-password/reset-password.ts) relies on Supabase's PASSWORD_RECOVERY auth state entered via the email link. It enforces a strength policy (length, uppercase, lowercase, number, symbol) shown as live checks, verifies the two fields match, then calls SupabaseService.client.auth.updateUser({ password }) (app/core/services/supabase.ts). It maps Supabase errors such as same-password and expired-link to friendly messages, and on success signs the user out globally and redirects to /login.

question: How is the upgrade-to-Pro banner shown to free users?
reference_answer: BannerService (app/core/services/banner.service.ts) derives a visible$ observable from ProfileService.profile$, emitting true only when the profile's plan is free and the questionnaire is completed (distinctUntilChanged). The PreviewBanner component (app/core/components/preview-banner/preview-banner.ts) binds to visible$ with the async pipe and, when the user clicks upgrade, opens the plan-picker modal on native platforms (Capacitor) or navigates to /pricing on the web.

question: How is the upgrade-success modal triggered and displayed after a purchase?
reference_answer: UpgradeModalService (app/core/services/upgrade-modal.service.ts) holds a BehaviorSubject of a { plan } object or null and exposes show(plan) and dismiss(). UpgradeSuccessModal (app/core/components/upgrade-success-modal/upgrade-success-modal.ts) subscribes to that state, displays the modal for the upgraded plan (Pro or Lifetime) with focus trapping, and its startTraining button dismisses the modal and navigates to /training. PaddleService calls show() after a completed checkout, which is what surfaces the modal.

question: How can a user switch between, edit, or create workout plans?
reference_answer: PlanSwitcherComponent (app/features/training/plan-switcher/plan-switcher.ts) loads the user's plans via WorkoutPlanService.getAllPlans (app/core/services/workout-plan.service.ts) alongside the built-in PRESET_PLANS, lets the user pick an active plan and emits planChanged. It also offers an edit modal (rename, change goal and experience, choose training days) and a multi-step custom-plan builder where each day is marked as a rest day or assigned muscle groups, all backed by the WorkoutPlan model and persisted through the service.

question: How are the Supabase and Paddle credentials and environment configured?
reference_answer: The Angular environment files hold the configuration: environments/environment.ts (development) and environment.prod.ts (production, swapped at build time via fileReplacements) expose a supabase block (url and anon key) and a paddle block (environment and client token). SupabaseService (app/core/services/supabase.ts) creates the Supabase client from environment.supabase, and PaddleService (app/core/services/paddle.service.ts) initializes Paddle.js from environment.paddle.
